title: Dolphin Cruise Review
meta_desc: Honest family review of Bay of Islands dolphin watching cruise, including prices, age suitability, and insider tips for a fun and memorable experience with kids.
intro: The Bay of Islands dolphin watching cruise is a must-do experience for many families visiting New Zealand. With prices starting from $60 per child and $120 per adult for a 3.5-hour cruise, it's an activity that promises to deliver an unforgettable encounter with these intelligent creatures. But what's it really like with kids, and is it worth the cost?

sections.heading: The Experience
sections.body:
The dolphin watching cruise in the Bay of Islands is an exhilarating experience, especially for kids. The 3.5-hour cruise takes you through the stunning bay, where you'll have the opportunity to see a resident pod of bottlenose dolphins and possibly other species like common dolphins. Most operators offer a swim-with option, but this is subject to the dolphins' cooperation and your child's age and ability.

Our kids (aged 6 and 8) absolutely loved the experience, and the crew were knowledgeable and friendly, providing interesting facts and insights about the dolphins and their habitat. The cruise is suitable for children aged 2 and above, but be prepared for potential sea sickness, especially if your child is prone to it.

Despite the potential downsides, we highly recommend this experience for families. The bay's resident pod of bottlenose dolphins ensures near-guaranteed sightings year-round, making it one of New Zealand's best wildlife experiences.

sections.heading: Value for Money and Insider Tips
sections.body:
While the cruise may seem expensive, especially for larger families, it's an experience that's hard to put a price on. The opportunity to see dolphins in their natural habitat, combined with the stunning scenery of the Bay of Islands, makes it a worthwhile investment.

To make the most of your experience, book your cruise for early morning or late afternoon when the dolphins are most active. Also, be sure to choose a reputable operator that prioritizes the welfare of the dolphins and the environment.
list: Book early morning or late afternoon cruises for best dolphin sightings
Choose a reputable operator that prioritizes dolphin welfare and the environment

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit and How to Book
sections.body:
The best time to visit the Bay of Islands for a dolphin watching cruise is during the summer months (December to February) when the weather is warm and sunny. However, the cruise operates year-round, and the bay's resident pod of bottlenose dolphins ensures near-guaranteed sightings regardless of the time of year.

To book your cruise, visit the operator's website or book through a local tour operator. Be sure to read reviews and check the operator's reputation before making a booking. You can also save money by booking a package deal that includes other activities or accommodations in the area.
list: Book through the operator's website or a local tour operator
Read reviews and check the operator's reputation before booking

sections.heading: Saving Money
sections.body:
While the dolphin watching cruise may seem expensive, there are ways to save money. Consider booking a package deal that includes other activities or accommodations in the area, or look for discounts and promotions on the operator's website.

Additionally, some operators offer discounts for children, students, or seniors, so be sure to ask about these when booking. By being flexible with your travel dates and booking in advance, you can also secure a better rate for your cruise.
list: Book a package deal that includes other activities or accommodations
Look for discounts and promotions on the operator's website
